channel.title: AI on Lucky Taorem | Tech & AI Blog
channel.description: Recent content in AI on Lucky Taorem | Tech & AI Blog

item.title: SambaNova Raises $1B
item.description:
Introduction to SambaNova and Its Recent Funding  

SambaNova, a leading AI chip maker, has recently raised $1 billion at a valuation of $11 billion, marking a significant milestone in the company’s growth trajectory. This funding round, led by General Atlantic, comes just five months after the company’s last mega round, demonstrating the immense interest and confidence investors have in SambaNova’s innovative approach to AI computing. As we delve into the details of this funding and the implications it holds for the AI industry, it’s essential to understand the context of AI startups and their rapid revenue growth, as seen in AI Startups Grow Revenue Fast.

item.title: ZML Unveils Free AI Inference Server
item.description:
Introduction to ZML and its Mission  

ZML, a French AI startup, has been making waves in the artificial intelligence community with its innovative approach to machine learning. Founded by Steeve Morin, ZML aims to break down existing silos in the AI industry and make different chips available for AI use cases at their maximum available speed. Recently, ZML released a free product called ZML/LLMD (LLM inference server) to speed inference across various AI chips. This move is expected to have a significant impact on the AI industry, and we will delve into the details of this release and its implications.

item.title: Vermilion Cliffs Ventures Fund II
item.description:
Introduction to Vermilion Cliffs Ventures  

Vermilion Cliffs Ventures, a venture capital firm founded by solo GP Ashley Smith, has announced the close of its second fund, Fund II, with a total of $25 million in capital commitments. This new fund will focus on investing in early-stage startups in the AI, security, and infrastructure spaces. As a seasoned marketing expert with experience working at companies like Twilio, Facebook, GitHub, and GitLab, Ashley Smith brings a unique perspective to the venture capital industry.

item.title: Prime Intellect Raises $130M
item.description:
Introduction to Prime Intellect  

Prime Intellect, a startup providing computing power and specialized software tools for building AI agents, has raised $130 million in Series A funding at a $1 billion valuation. This significant investment is a testament to the growing demand for AI solutions in the enterprise sector. As Anthropic’s Fable 5: The AI Safety Crisis highlights, the development of AI agents is a critical area of focus for many organizations.

item.title: Tech Layoffs Surge
item.description:
Introduction to the Tech Layoff Crisis  

The tech industry has been witnessing a significant surge in layoffs, with many major companies citing Artificial Intelligence (AI) as the primary reason for these cuts. Despite reporting record revenues, companies like Oracle, Google, and Meta have been reducing their workforce, leaving many to wonder about the true motives behind these decisions. In this article, we will delve into the details of the recent tech layoffs, analyzing the role of AI in these decisions and the potential implications for the industry as a whole.

item.title: Anthropic AI Feud
item.description:
Introduction to Anthropic’s AI Model Feud  

The recent feud between Anthropic, an AI startup, and the US government has sparked a heated debate in the tech community. At the center of this controversy is Anthropic’s AI model, Mythos, which the company claims is so advanced that it poses a global cybersecurity threat. In an effort to mitigate this risk, Anthropic released a modified version of the model, called Fable, which it deemed safer for public use. However, the US government intervened, placing export controls on Fable and prompting Anthropic to revoke access to both models.

item.title: Apple News Update
item.description:
Introduction to Apple’s Latest Updates  

Apple has been at the forefront of technological innovation, constantly pushing the boundaries of what is possible with their devices and software. From the iPhone to the Mac, Apple’s products have become an integral part of our daily lives. Recently, Apple has released several updates and new features that are set to revolutionize the way we interact with our devices. In this article, we will delve into the details of these updates and explore what they mean for Apple users.

item.title: OpenAI Unveils Patch
item.description:
Introduction to OpenAI’s Latest Initiative  

OpenAI has recently made several significant announcements focused on enhancing cybersecurity, particularly for open-source projects. The company has introduced an improved version of its security-specialized model, GPT-5.5-Cyber, and has expanded its international collaborations with governments and institutions to provide them with “trusted access” to its latest cybersecurity models. Moreover, OpenAI has released its Codex Security scanner as an app plug-in. However, the most notable initiative is the launch of “Patch the Planet,” a project aimed at supporting open-source maintainers in strengthening their code bases and incorporating AI security tools into their development process.

item.title: MacBooks Now Affordable
item.description:
Introduction to Apple Silicon and MacBook Affordability  

The era of Apple Silicon has revolutionized the MacBook lineup, making these devices more affordable than ever. With the introduction of Apple’s own silicon chips, the company has been able to optimize performance, power efficiency, and cost. As a result, consumers can now purchase a MacBook Air with 512GB of storage and 16GB of memory for $1099 directly from Apple. This is a significant reduction in price compared to just a few years ago, when a similar configuration would have cost $1599.

item.title: Apple's AI Boost
item.description:
Introduction to Apple’s AI Enhancements  

Apple’s upcoming operating systems are creating a significant buzz in the tech world, and for good reason. The new Siri AI is undoubtedly the star of the show, but it’s not the only AI-related enhancement that Apple has in store for its users. In this article, we’ll be taking a closer look at some of the less spectacular but still useful AI enhancements that Apple has introduced in its productivity apps and other software.

item.title: ChatGPT Prompts Mastery
item.description:
Introduction to ChatGPT and Prompt Engineering  

ChatGPT, Google Gemini, and other AI-powered tools are revolutionizing the way we interact with technology. These chatbots can compose sonnets, write code, get philosophical, and automate tasks, making artificial intelligence accessible to the masses. However, to get the most out of these tools, it’s essential to master the art of prompt engineering. Prompt engineering is a specialized skill that involves crafting input prompts to elicit specific, useful, and interesting responses from AI chatbots. In this article, we’ll delve into the world of prompt engineering, exploring techniques, tips, and examples to help you unlock ChatGPT’s full potential.

item.title: Trump Admin Cracks Down
item.description:
Introduction to the Trump Administration’s Crackdown on Anthropic  

The recent news of the Trump administration’s crackdown on Anthropic, a leading AI lab, has sent shockwaves throughout the tech industry. The administration’s decision to force Anthropic to take its two newest AI models offline has sparked a heated debate about AI policy and digital sovereignty. In this article, we will delve into the details of the crackdown, its implications for the broader AI ecosystem, and the potential beneficiaries of this move.

item.title: Siri AI Revolution: Apple's Smart Travel Assistant
item.description: When you’re out hiking, testing beta features, or just craving a moment of digital connection, the way Siri has evolved can shift from a novelty to a necessity. In this deep dive, we explore the remarkable journey of Apple’s Siri AI—from a struggling voice assistant to a highly personalized travel guide, powering your day like a middle-man, and redefining what conversational AI can do. Whether you’re walking across the Golden Gate Bridge or planning your next hotspot in San Francisco, this blog unpacks the tech, features, and implications of Apple’s latest AI breakthrough.

item.title: Kyber: The Secret Tech Powering Millions of Future Robots
item.description:
The Man Behind VLC Is Now Building the Backbone of Physical AI  

Jean-Baptiste Kempf is a name that may not ring a bell for the average internet user, but his work has touched nearly every person on the planet. As the lead developer of VLC Media Player, Kempf helped create one of the most ubiquitous pieces of open-source software in history, with over 6 billion downloads to date. Now, he’s turning his attention to a challenge that could be even more transformative: building the infrastructure that will power millions of robots and drones in the real world.

item.title: AI Boost
item.description:
Introduction to Adobe’s AI-Powered Firefly  

Adobe has been at the forefront of innovation in the creative industry, and its latest move is a testament to this commitment. The company is updating its Firefly AI assistant with new capabilities and integrating it into some of its most popular applications, including Premiere, Illustrator, InDesign, and Frame.io. This integration is set to revolutionize the way creatives work, offering them unprecedented levels of efficiency, inspiration, and precision.

item.title: AI Evolved
item.description:
Introduction to Embodied AI and World Models  

The field of artificial intelligence (AI) has witnessed tremendous growth in recent years, with various approaches being explored to create more sophisticated and human-like intelligent systems. One such approach is embodied AI, which focuses on developing AI systems that can interact with and understand their environment through sensory experiences. Another crucial aspect of AI research is the development of world models, which enable AI systems to learn about the world and make predictions about future events. A startup has been making waves in the AI community by training embodied AI and world models using a vast dataset of 2 billion videos per year from 10 million monthly active users.

item.title: Midjourney Revolutionizes AI Art
item.description:
Midjourney goes from generating cat images to full-body ultrasound scans  

In a surprising move, Midjourney CEO David Holz has announced the company’s first hardware product - The Midjourney Scanner, an ultrasound-based full-body scanner. This is a significant departure from the company’s previous focus on generating cat pictures using its AI image generator. But what does this new development mean for the future of healthcare technology?

item.title: Photoshop and Premiere now have AI assistants
item.description:
Adobe’s plan to integrate its AI-powered chatbots into all its Creative Cloud suite applications has finally come to fruition. The tech giant has officially launched a public beta for its AI Assistants, giving users access to AI-powered chatbots in Photoshop, Premiere, Illustrator, InDesign, and Frame.io. This move aims to revolutionize the way designers and editors work, making tasks easier, faster, and more efficient.

What are AI Assistants in Adobe Creative Cloud?  

Adobe’s AI Assistants are bespoke chatbots designed to aid users in their creative workflows. These AI-powered tools can perform a wide range of tasks, from automating repetitive tasks to offering expert-level advice and guidance. With the AI Assistants, users can now focus on the creative aspects of their work, leaving the mundane tasks to the AI.

item.title: How to turn off AI in your Google Docs
item.description:
Introduction to AI in Google Docs  

Google Docs has become an essential tool for individuals and businesses alike, providing a convenient and collaborative way to create and edit documents. Recently, Google introduced a new feature called “Gemini” which utilizes artificial intelligence (AI) to assist with writing tasks. While this feature can be helpful, some users may find the frequent “write with Gemini” pop-ups to be annoying and distracting. In this article, we will explore the reasons behind these pop-ups and provide a step-by-step guide on how to turn them off.
